session.user:
# /erk:plan-implement
Implement a plan - either from GitHub, a markdown file, an existing impl directory under `.erk/impl-context/`, or by saving the current plan first.
This is the primary implementation workflow - it orchestrates:
1. Setting up the impl directory under `.erk/impl-context/<branch>/` (from issue, file, existing folder, or fresh plan) 2. Executing the implementation 3. Running CI and submitting the PR
## Prerequisites
- Must be in a git repository managed by erk - GitHub CLI (`gh`) must be authenticated - One of: - A plan number, URL, or file path argument - An existing impl directory under `.erk/impl-context/` - A plan branch checked out (e.g., `plnd/...` or `P{number}-...`) - A plan in `~/.claude/plans/` (from plan mode)
## Usage
```bash /erk:plan-implement # Use impl directory, detect from branch, or save current plan /erk:plan-implement 2521 # Fetch and implement plan #2521 /erk:plan-implement https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/2521 # URL form /erk:plan-implement ./my-plan.md # Implement from local markdown file ```
---
## CRITICAL: PR Operations
**NEVER** run direct GitHub CLI commands for PR operations during implementation:
- `gh pr edit` — destroys plan-header metadata block - `gh pr ready` — bypasses lifecycle tracking - `gh pr close` / `gh pr merge`
All PR operations are handled by `erk pr submit` in Step 13. It automatically:
- Generates the PR title and body - Preserves the plan-header metadata block - Marks draft PRs as ready for review - Updates lifecycle stage
## Agent Instructions
### Step 1: Set Up Implementation
Parse `` and run the consolidated setup command:
- **If numeric** (e.g., `2521`): `erk exec setup-impl --issue 2521` - **If GitHub URL** (e.g., `https://github.com/.../issues/2521`): Extract number, `erk exec setup-impl --issue 2521` - **If path to file** (anything else non-empty): `erk exec setup-impl --file <path>` - **If empty**: `erk exec setup-impl` (auto-detects from `.erk/impl-context/`, branch, or fails)
```bash erk exec setup-impl [--issue <N> | --file <path>] ```
This single command handles:
- Fetching plan from GitHub (draft PR) - Setting up from a local markdown file - Auto-detecting from existing impl directory under `.erk/impl-context/` - Auto-detecting plan number from branch name (P{number}-... or PR lookup) - Creating/checking out the feature branch - Creating impl directory under `.erk/impl-context/<branch>/` with plan content - Running impl-init validation - Cleaning up `.erk/impl-context/` staging directory (git rm + commit + push)
Otherwise, generate a branch slug and set up from the specified issue:
1. Fetch the title: `gh pr view <ISSUE_ARG> --json title -q .title` 2. Generate a branch slug from the title: - 2-4 hyphenated lowercase words, max 30 characters - Capture distinctive essence, drop filler words (the, a, for, implementation, plan) - Prefer action verbs: add, fix, refactor, update, consolidate, extract, migrate - Examples: "fix-auth-session", "add-plan-validation", "refactor-gateway-abc" 3. Store as `BRANCH_SLUG`.
```bash erk exec setup-impl --issue <ISSUE_ARG> --branch-slug="${BRANCH_SLUG}" ```
If `setup-impl` exits with code 1 and `error: "no_plan_found"`, fall back to saving the current plan:
Generate a branch slug from the plan title (the first `# ` heading in the plan file):
- 2-4 hyphenated lowercase words, max 30 characters - Capture distinctive essence, drop filler words (the, a, for, implementation, plan) - Prefer action verbs: add, fix, refactor, update, consolidate, extract, migrate - Store as `BRANCH_SLUG`
Save the current plan to GitHub and capture the plan number:
```bash erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="66d5811b-b81c-4ec7-9b85-be8f06ecba93" --branch-slug="${BRANCH_SLUG}" ```
Parse the JSON output to get `plan_number`, then:
```bash erk exec setup-impl --issue <plan_number> ```
The `setup-impl` output includes `related_docs` (skills and docs to load) and `has_plan_tracking` (whether GitHub issue tracking is active).
If this fails, display the error and stop.
### Step 2: Read Plan and Load Context
Read `plan.md` from the impl directory set up in Step 1 to understand:
- Overall goal and context - Context & Understanding sections (API quirks, architectural insights, pitfalls) - Implementation phases and dependencies - Success criteria
**Context Consumption**: Plans contain expensive discoveries. Ignoring `[CRITICAL:]` tags, "Related Context:" subsections, or "DO NOT" items causes repeated mistakes.
### Step 3: Load Related Documentation
If plan contains "Related Documentation" section, load listed skills via Skill tool and read listed docs.
### Step 4: Create TodoWrite Entries
Create todo entries for each phase from the plan.
### Step 5: Signal GitHub Started
```bash erk exec impl-signal started --session-id="66d5811b-b81c-4ec7-9b85-be8f06ecba93" 2>/dev/null || true ```
This also deletes the Claude plan file (from `~/.claude/plans/`) since:
- The content has been saved to GitHub - The content has been snapshotted to `.erk/scratch/` - Keeping it could cause confusion if the user tries to re-save
### Step 6: Execute Each Phase Sequentially
For each phase:
1. **Mark phase as in_progress** (in TodoWrite) 2. **Read task requirements** carefully 3. **Implement code AND tests together**: - Load `dignified-python-313` skill for coding standards - Load `fake-driven-testing` skill for test patterns - Follow project AGENTS.md standards 4. **Mark phase as completed** (in TodoWrite) 5. **Report progress**: changes made, what's next
**Important:** `plan.md` is immutable - NEVER edit during implementation
### Step 7: Report Progress
After each phase: report changes made and what's next.
### Step 8: Final Verification
Confirm all tasks executed, success criteria met, note deviations, summarize changes.
### Step 9: Signal GitHub Ended
```bash erk exec impl-signal ended --session-id="66d5811b-b81c-4ec7-9b85-be8f06ecba93" 2>/dev/null || true ```
### Step 10: Upload Session for Async Learn
```bash erk exec upload-impl-session --session-id="66d5811b-b81c-4ec7-9b85-be8f06ecba93" 2>/dev/null || true ```
This reads plan reference from the impl directory, captures session info, and uploads for async learn processing.
### Step 11: Clean Up Impl Context
Remove the `.erk/impl-context/` directory from the repo:
```bash erk exec cleanup-impl-context ```
This removes `.erk/impl-context/`, stages the deletion, commits, and pushes — all idempotent.
### Step 12: Run CI Iteratively
1. If `.erk/prompt-hooks/post-plan-implement-ci.md` exists: follow its instructions 2. Otherwise: check CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md for CI commands
### Step 13: Submit PR
Push the branch and create or update the PR using the Graphite-aware submit pipeline:
```bash erk pr submit ```
This handles pushing commits, creating/updating the PR, generating the PR description, and enhancing with Graphite stack metadata when available.
After successful submission, signal lifecycle transition:
```bash erk exec impl-signal submitted 2>/dev/null || true ```
Then validate PR completion invariants:
```bash erk pr check --stage=impl ```
This validates PR submission readiness including implementation-specific checks (e.g., `.erk/impl-context/` must be cleaned up). If checks fail, display output and warn user.
### Step 14: Output Format
- **Start**: "Setting up implementation..." or "Fetching plan #X..." - **After setup**: "Implementation environment ready, reading plan..." - **Each phase**: "Phase X: [brief description]" with code changes - **End**: "Plan execution complete. [Summary]"
---
## Related Commands
- `/erk:plan-save` - Save plan only, don't implement (for defer-to-later workflow) - `/erk:replan` - Re-plan an existing plan with current codebase state
session.assistant: I'll implement the plan on the current branch. Starting with setup.

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Set up implementation environment from current branch

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Set up implementation environment from current branch

session.assistant: Good, the implementation environment is ready for plan #8961. Let me read the plan to understand the work:
session.tool_result:
1→# Update CHANGELOG.md Unreleased Section 2→ 3→## Context 4→ 5→The CHANGELOG.md unreleased section is synced to `7d49225f8`. Since then, 34 commits have landed on master. After filtering out internal refactors, test infra, build tooling, already-documented commits, and erk-dev commands, 6 user-visible changes warrant changelog entries. 6→ 7→## File to Modify 8→ 9→- `CHANGELOG.md` — Unreleased section only 10→ 11→## Changes 12→ 13→### 1. Update "As of" marker 14→ 15→Change: 16→``` 17→<!-- As of: 7d49225f8 --> 18→``` 19→To: 20→``` 21→<!-- As of: f1268fd27 --> 22→``` 23→ 24→### 2. Add to Added section (1 entry) 25→ 26→Append after the last existing Added entry: 27→``` 28→- Add Check 8 to `erk objective check` for roadmap table sync validation: detects when the prose roadmap table has drifted from YAML source of truth (20f3cd393) 29→``` 30→
... [20 lines omitted] ...
session.assistant: Signal GitHub that implementation has started:
Now let me read the CHANGELOG.md to see the current structure:
session.tool_result:
1→# Changelog 2→ 3→All notable changes to erk will be documented in this file. 4→ 5→The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.1.0/), 6→and this project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html). 7→ 8→## [Unreleased] 9→ 10→<!-- As of: 7d49225f8 --> 11→ 12→### Added 13→ 14→- Support running `erk one-shot --repo owner/repo` to dispatch tasks to remote repositories without a local git clone (c38a10fd, 83ba44ce, 70e592342) 15→- Codespace connect auto-checks out local branch in remote environment (9aa2d881) 16→- Add objective node management: update fields and add new nodes to existing phases (74f36b32) 17→- Wire incremental dispatch into TUI with plan input modal (e94dabcf) 18→- Hide stub branches from `erk br list` by default, with `--all/-a` flag to show them (578cc981) 19→- Make TUI help screen view-mode-aware, showing context-sensitive shortcuts for plans vs objectives (433c9c2e) 20→ 21→### Changed 22→ 23→- Show conflicted files and prompt for confirmation before launching Claude in `erk pr rebase` (c0461e8a) 24→- Sort command palette menu items alphabetically within each subgroup (94957ecb) 25→ 26→### Fixed 27→ 28→- Fix land learn pipeline to fetch remote sessions from async-learn branches when local sessions are missing (2db54844) 29→ 30→## [0.9.9] - 2026-03-06 16:54 PT
... [70 lines omitted] ...
38→- Add artifact allowlist to suppress `erk doctor` warnings for intentionally-modified artifacts 49→- Fix `erk doctor` hiding warnings behind green checkmarks in condensed mode 56→- Store CI failure summaries as persistent PR comments with fast-path fetch, reducing TUI summary load from 4 API calls to 1
session.assistant: Now I'll make the updates. First, update the "As of" marker:
session.tool_result: The file /Users/schrockn/code/erk/CHANGELOG.md has been updated successfully.
session.assistant: Now add the new entry to the Added section:

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param:
- Make TUI help screen view-mode-aware, showing context-sensitive shortcuts for plans vs objectives (433c9c2e)
### Changed

- Make TUI help screen view-mode-aware, showing context-sensitive shortcuts for plans vs o...[truncated 84 chars]...hen the prose roadmap table has drifted from YAML source of truth (20f3cd393)
### Changed

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param:
- Show conflicted files and prompt for confirmation before launching Claude in `erk pr rebase` (c0461e8a) - Sort command palette menu items alphabetically within each subgroup (94957ecb)

- Show conflicted files and prompt for confirmation before launching Claude in `erk pr reb...[truncated 575 chars]...date `/erk:pr-rebase` skill to use AskUserQuestion for push options (932de4cca)

session.tool_result: The file /Users/schrockn/code/erk/CHANGELOG.md has been updated successfully.
session.assistant: Let me verify the changes by reading the updated section:
session.tool_result:
1→# Changelog 2→ 3→All notable changes to erk will be documented in this file. 4→ 5→The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.1.0/), 6→and this project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html). 7→ 8→## [Unreleased] 9→ 10→<!-- As of: f1268fd27 --> 11→ 12→### Added 13→ 14→- Support running `erk one-shot --repo owner/repo` to dispatch tasks to remote repositories without a local git clone (c38a10fd, 83ba44ce, 70e592342) 15→- Codespace connect auto-checks out local branch in remote environment (9aa2d881) 16→- Add objective node management: update fields and add new nodes to existing phases (74f36b32) 17→- Wire incremental dispatch into TUI with plan input modal (e94dabcf) 18→- Hide stub branches from `erk br list` by default, with `--all/-a` flag to show them (578cc981) 19→- Make TUI help screen view-mode-aware, showing context-sensitive shortcuts for plans vs objectives (433c9c2e) 20→- Add Check 8 to `erk objective check` for roadmap table sync validation: detects when the prose roadmap table has drifted from YAML source of truth (20f3cd393) 21→ 22→### Changed 23→ 24→- Show conflicted files and prompt for confirmation before launching Claude in `erk pr rebase` (c0461e8a) 25→- Sort command palette menu items alphabetically within each subgroup (94957ecb) 26→- Remove `--sync` from `erk pr checkout` (now purely local); add `--script` and `--sync` options to `erk pr teleport` for remote Graphite submission workflows (7065d4546) 27→- Rename bundled slash command `/erk:rebase` to `/erk:pr-rebase` for consistency with PR-focused command naming (51d6ccb84) 28→- `erk objective view` now infers the objective from the current branch when no explicit reference is provided (56dbf1a04) 29→- Add arrow key navigation to the objective nodes screen in TUI (91d3f1143) 30→- Update `/erk:pr-rebase` skill to use AskUserQuestion for push options (932de4cca)
... [15 lines omitted] ...
44→- Add artifact allowlist to suppress `erk doctor` warnings for intentionally-modified artifacts
session.assistant:
Perfect! Verification complete: - ✅ "As of" marker updated to f1268fd27 - ✅ Added section has 7 entries (6 existing + 1 new) - ✅ Changed section has 7 entries (2 existing + 5 new) - ✅ Fixed section unchanged
Now commit the changes:

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param:
git add CHANGELOG.md && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF' Update CHANGELOG.md Unreleased sectio...[truncated 250 chars]...61
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com> EOF )" && git log --oneline -1

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Upload implementation session for async learn processing
